Package org.areslib.math.filter

Class KalmanFilter1D

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.math.filter.KalmanFilter1D
public class KalmanFilter1D extends Object
A lightweight, dependency-free 1-Dimensional Kalman Filter for state tracking.

Optimally tracks a 1D state consisting of [Position, Velocity] by merging a mathematical predictive model with noisy sensor measurements (e.g., encoders). This is particularly effective for flywheels and elevators where low-pass filters introduce unacceptable phase lag.

This implementation uses explicit algebraic expansions of the standard 2x2 Kalman equations, completely avoiding matrix object allocations to ensure 0 GC overhead during loops.

    Constructors

    Description
    KalmanFilter1D(double qPos, double qVel)
    Constructs a 1D Kalman Filter.

  • Constructor Details

    • KalmanFilter1D

      public KalmanFilter1D(double qPos, double qVel)
      Constructs a 1D Kalman Filter.
      Parameters:
      qPos - Expected variance in the position model (e.g., 0.001).
      qVel - Expected variance in the velocity model (e.g., 0.1).

  • Method Details

    • reset

      public void reset(double initialPos, double initialVel)
      Resets the filter to a known state.
      Parameters:
      initialPos - Initial position.
      initialVel - Initial velocity.

    • predict

      public void predict(double dtSeconds)
      Predicts the next state using the kinematic model. Must be called once per loop before updating.
      Parameters:
      dtSeconds - The time elapsed since the last prediction.

    • updatePosition

      public void updatePosition(double measurement, double rVariance)
      Updates the state with a new position measurement.
      Parameters:
      measurement - The noisy position reading.
      rVariance - The variance (noise) of the measurement.

    • updateVelocity

      public void updateVelocity(double measurement, double rVariance)
      Updates the state with a new velocity measurement.
      Parameters:
      measurement - The noisy velocity reading.
      rVariance - The variance (noise) of the measurement.

    • getPosition

      public double getPosition()
      Returns the optimally filtered position.
      Returns:
      The optimally filtered position.

    • getVelocity

      public double getVelocity()
      Returns the optimally filtered velocity.
      Returns:
      The optimally filtered velocity.
